title: Question about Evangelion Soundtracks
wikitext:
[]: Question about Evangelion Soundtracks...             Quote Post by Eva Wolf » Thu Mar 31, 2011 5:18 pm Hello everyone, First time poster, and I recently got back into Evangelion with the recent releases of the Rebuild films and want to invest some money in the music side of the series. Now, apparently the soundtracks for Evangelion have never been released in America but I have found three albums/sets that I believe will cover all the music from the series as a whole. I just wanted to check in to make sure I am not missing anything. I plan on buying the two albums for the Rebuild films, as well as the S2 Set for the Evangelion original series. Between those three, will I be missing out on any good tracks? In addition, will those albums display in English on my computer, or more likely, if they do not, where can I go to find track names to manually type in for my convenience? Thanks to anyone who answers.

[]:             Quote Post by Travosaga » Thu Mar 31, 2011 7:07 pm I've purchased a couple soundtracks such as the Xenosaga soundtrack and the tracks come up as a mix of Japanese and English. Some of the titles have English in them...in fact most did now that I'm looking at it. I'm not sure where to get translations of the names that are in total Japanese though. I guess you could use google translator as a last resort lol. The ones you bought should cover everything you need. The only thing I would check for is if the rebuild soundtracks come with the produced song pieces with the actual recording artists. It might only contain the orchestra pieces.

[]:             Quote Post by Alastor » Thu Mar 31, 2011 10:20 pm I just checked my copy of Genesis of Aquarion OST 2, an import, it won't translate beyond the ones already in english. Or at least in Windows Media Player/Windows XP.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Wolf » Sat Apr 02, 2011 10:23 pm Thank you both for the replies, and sorry for being so late with it.

[]:             Quote Post by Alastor » Sat Apr 02, 2011 10:46 pm Forgot this: It'll only read the files as "Track(Number here)" when you try to open the files manually, as such "Sousei no Aquarion", a track from Aquarion OST1 was rendered as "Track1".

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Apr 02, 2011 11:06 pm Isn't this more of a CDDB issue and not with the CD itself? Track information isn't encoded in the CD, there are databases that use the track number and lengths to "ID" a CD and match it to find track information. I know windows media player uses some Microsoft specific database, there's also a couple of free public databases. I have an import Aria CD that Windows recognizes but all the song names are still in Japanese. You can enter in the information manually, you just need to figure out how, each media player is going to be different in this respect. If you are ripping the CD into mp3's the mp3 itself has metadata fields for artist, album, song, track #'s, etc. When you rip the CD you should be able to manually edit this information.

[]:             Quote Post by rhfxz1s » Sun Apr 03, 2011 12:53 am Ornette wrote: Isn't this more of a CDDB issue and not with the CD itself? Track information isn't encoded in the CD, there are databases that use the track number and lengths to "ID" a CD and match it to find track information. I know windows media player uses some Microsoft specific database, there's also a couple of free public databases. I have an import Aria CD that Windows recognizes but all the song names are still in Japanese. You can enter in the information manually, you just need to figure out how, each media player is going to be different in this respect. If you are ripping the CD into mp3's the mp3 itself has metadata fields for artist, album, song, track #'s, etc. When you rip the CD you should be able to manually edit this information.
